title: urandra corniculata
intro: Urandra corniculata, also known as Indian snakeroot or Picrorhiza, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, belonging to the family Gentianaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective effects, due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as picrorhizin and icariin. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Urandra corniculata can be prepared in various ways, including as a decoction, tincture, or powder, which can be used to treat conditions such as liver and kidney problems, digestive issues, and respiratory infections.
